title: What Has My Body Managed This Week?
subtitle: A journal practice for recognising the body's work rather than only its failures
excerpt: A journal practice for acknowledging what the body has done this week, not just what it hasn't. For the pattern of only noticing the body when it fails, hurts, or falls short of expectations.

practice.whenToUse: Use on a Friday or weekend, or after a week in which you have been critical of your body, to rebalance the ledger.

content.text: A journal practice for noticing what the body has done this week rather than what it has failed to do. The body carries a lot. This practice asks for an account of it.
List five things your body did this week that required physical capacity.
Not exercise. Ordinary things: standing at a sink, carrying bags, keeping someone upright during a long day, being somewhere for a long time.
Where did you take your body this week that was difficult?
A difficult conversation, a bad night, a long journey, a hard piece of physical work. Write what the body was present for.
What did you notice your body asking for this week that you did not give it?
No self-criticism. Just an inventory. What signals came that you overrode.
What is one thing your body asked for that you did give it?
Even if it was small. Rest, a meal, a moment of quiet. Write what you gave.
